title: Sprint 3-inherited tautology: select_past_note 'integration' tests reconstruct the handler's logic instead of invoking it
description: Three tests claim coverage of REQ-FEED-024 (the editing_session_state_changed emit from select_past_note): test_select_past_note_emits_editing_session_state_changed (line 109), test_select_past_note_editing_payload_contains_body (line 176), and test_select_past_note_nonexistent_body_is_empty (line 248). NONE of these tests invoke the select_past_note Tauri command. They each manually replicate the handler's body-extraction logic (lines 124-151, 196-214, 248-264) by calling scan_vault_feed, then invoking parse_markdown_to_blocks → compose_state_for_select_past_note → make_editing_state_changed_payload directly. The actual select_past_note in feed.rs:243-272 is never executed by these tests. Consequently, regressions in feed.rs::select_past_note (e.g., missing parse_markdown_to_blocks call, incorrect note_metadata key handling, wrong emit ordering, or absent editing_session_state_changed emit) would NOT cause these tests to fail — they only validate the helper composition that the test authors choose to invoke. This is a known Sprint 3 antipattern (FIND-S3-101 medium, marked 'open' tradeoff for AppHandle limitation), but Sprint 4 inherits it and adds new tests (PROP-FEED-S4-012/013/014) that follow the same shape. EC-FEED-017 emit ordering (editing_session_state_changed before feed_state_changed) is acknowledged in Sprint 3's gate as 'verified by code review only' — this remains an integration coverage gap that Sprint 4 did not close.
remediation: Either (a) introduce a mock AppHandle / Emitter trait to drive feed.rs::select_past_note end-to-end and assert on captured events (preferred), or (b) refactor select_past_note so its observable IPC composition is in a separately testable pure function with full keyset coverage that the tests genuinely exercise (rather than the helper-by-helper approach which lets handler regressions slip through). At minimum add an emit-order assertion to lock down EC-FEED-017.
